title: Is it a VOWEL or CONSONANT

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to take a character (C)(C)(C) as input and check whether the given character is a vowel or a consonant.

NOTE:−NOTE:-NOTE:−
Vowels are 'A', 'E', 'I', 'O', 'U'. Rest all alphabets are called consonants.


Input Format

First line will contain the character CCC.


Output Format
Print "Vowel" if the given character is a vowel, otherwise print "Consonant".
constraints: CCC is an uppercase English alphabet
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single character CCC, which is an uppercase English alphabet.
content.outputFormat: Print 'Vowel' if the given character is a vowel, otherwise print 'Consonant'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The character 'Z' is not one of the vowels ('A', 'E', 'I', 'O', 'U'), so it is a consonant.
The character 'A' is one of the vowels, so it is a vowel.
The character 'B' is not one of the vowels, so it is a consonant.

solution.approach: The approach is to check if the given character is one of the vowels ('A', 'E', 'I', 'O', 'U'). If it is, print 'Vowel', otherwise print 'Consonant'. This can be done using a simple if-else statement or a switch statement.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) — The time complexity is constant because we are only checking a single character.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — The space complexity is constant because we are not using any additional space that scales with the input size.
